content:
Key Findings

  • Hyndburn Borough Council spent a total of £211.3M across 29,804 transactions from 2016/17 to 2025/26.
  • The council's spending data covers a comprehensive 10-year period, providing a significant dataset for analysis.
  • No specific anomalies or issues were flagged in the automated analysis of Hyndburn's spending data.
  • The data includes transactions from a variety of suppliers, though specific names are not provided in the dataset.
  • The total spend of £211.3M reflects the council's financial activity over a decade.

Understanding Hyndburn's Spending Data

Hyndburn Borough Council's published spending data offers a detailed look into the financial transactions of a district council in Lancashire. Covering a decade from 2016/17 to 2025/26, the data includes £211.3M in expenditures across 29,804 transactions. This comprehensive dataset provides valuable insights into the council's financial management and priorities.

The data's breadth, encompassing 10 financial years, allows for a thorough analysis of spending patterns and trends. While specific supplier names are not detailed in the dataset, the volume of transactions suggests a diverse range of suppliers and services utilised by the council. This diversity is typical for a district council managing various public services and infrastructure projects.

Analysing the Data for Anomalies

Automated analysis of Hyndburn's spending data did not flag any significant issues or anomalies. This includes checks for duplicates, split payments, round-number anomalies, and adherence to Benford's Law. The absence of red flags indicates a level of consistency and regularity in the council's financial transactions. However, the lack of specific supplier names limits the depth of analysis that can be conducted on individual transactions.

The total spend of £211.3M over a decade reflects the council's ongoing commitments to public services, infrastructure, and community projects. While the data does not highlight any immediate concerns, it serves as a foundation for further investigation and transparency. The council's adherence to the Local Government Transparency Code ensures that this data is accessible to the public, promoting accountability and informed civic engagement.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Hyndburn Borough Council about specific spending items or suppliers not detailed in the published data.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to gain insights into the decision-making process behind the council's financial transactions.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see detailed information about contracts awarded and their financial implications.
  • Write to your local councillor to express any concerns or questions about the council's spending and request further clarification on specific transactions.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and conduct your own analysis of Hyndburn Borough Council's financial transactions.
This article is based on Hyndburn Borough Council's published spending data covering 2016/17 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
